reasoning: In 2024, the EU imported around 12.5-13 bcm of Russian natural gas per quarter. However, the transit route through Ukraine (which supplied around 14-15 bcm annually, or ~3.5 bcm per quarter) was shut down in January 2025. Without the Ukrainian route, the remaining infrastructure for Russian gas to the EU consists mainly of the TurkStream pipeline (max capacity to Europe ~15 bcm/yr) and liquefied natural gas (LNG) deliveries (around 18-20 bcm/yr). This effectively caps the quarterly potential around 9-10 bcm, structurally preventing a jump to 15 bcm. Recent news from 2025 confirms imports hit an all-time low in Q1 and Q2. Given the community forecast of 1% and hard physical capacity constraints, reaching 15 bcm in any quarter of 2025 is extremely unlikely.
